slices.focus: Storm damage repairs and deepened empathy for client costs
slices.summary: Heavy rain caused subfloor damage requiring repair and yard drainage regrading, thinning already strained savings and delaying vacation plans. The experience deepened empathy for clients hesitating on uncertain mitigation costs — the fear of an open-ended expense is the real barrier. Plans to use a known/variable/unknown cost categorization approach in workshops to help clients frame the uncertainty rather than freeze on it.
open_loops: Check for other hidden water damage in the house beyond the visible subfloor
Preserve some quality of life within the tighter budget rather than cutting everything to bare obligations
Build a cost-range reference page for clients — idea noted but not yet implemented
decisions: Tighten household spending, prioritize rebuilding the savings buffer first
Postpone the long trip; substitute a nearby overnight to preserve some relief without the cost
Use known/variable/unknown cost categories in workshops to help clients understand and act despite uncertainty
